# Patriots' Pride Windows — Veteran-Owned Window, Door & Siding Installer (TN, GA & AL)

> Patriots' Pride Windows is a veteran-owned, 4th-generation window, door and siding installation company headquartered in Chattanooga, Tennessee, with a second office in metro Atlanta, Georgia. The company serves 109 cities across Tennessee, Georgia and Alabama — the Southeast Tennessee tri-state region, Northeast Alabama, and the North Georgia / metro Atlanta corridor. Andersen Certified and James Hardie Alliance Select. Slogan: "No homeowner left behind."

## Company Information
- Name: Patriots' Pride Windows
- Legal name: JudRut Consulting Firm LLC (DBA Patriots' Pride Windows)
- Type: Home & Construction Business / Window, Door & Siding Installer
- Owners: Judson Simpson and Rutger Conradi (veteran-owned, owner-operated)
- Founded: 2021
- Location: 5036 Hwy 58 Ste 102, Chattanooga, TN 37416
- Second office: Metro Atlanta, Georgia
- Phone: (423) 830-7138
- Email: info@patriotspridewindows.com
- Website: https://www.patriotspridewindows.com
- License: TN #11022
- Insurance: $2,000,000 liability + workers' compensation
- Google rating: 5.0 stars (118 reviews)
- Projects completed: 15,000+
- Awards: Best of the Best 2024 & 2025 — Chattanooga Times Free Press
- Associations: BNI, NARI

## Differentiators
- True 4th-generation install company, NOT a sales/marketing organization.
- No 10-step sales pitch, no multi-hour in-home sales presentation; one honest, itemized price.
- Will install owner-supplied windows and doors (product bought elsewhere) to manufacturer spec — most competitors refuse.
- Manufacturer-direct on every brand. Signed timeline agreements between both parties.
- Owner-operators handle both front-end and backend so nothing falls through the cracks.

## Services Offered
### Window Replacement & New Construction Windows
- Styles: Single-Hung, Double-Hung, Casement, Picture, Bay, Bow, Awning, Arched, Palladian, Custom
- Product series: Patriot 3000 and Patriot 7400 (vinyl and composite)
- Energy Star certified; Low-E, argon-filled insulated glass; reinforced frames that won't warp, fade or crack
- Brands: Andersen, MI, Polaris, Vinyl Max, Windsor, Sunrise, HGI
- Price range: $700–$2,200 per window; install 1–2 days; Lifetime material & labor warranty (transferable once, then prorated)

### Door Replacement (Entry, Patio, French, Storm)
- Full configuration catalog; fiberglass and steel
- Brands: ProVia, Andersen
- Price range: $3,973–$7,500; install 1–2 days; 15-year material & labor warranty
- Hometown Hero: 50% off exterior doors for active military, veterans, police, fire, EMS, dispatchers

### Siding Replacement (full replacement only)
- Materials: James Hardie fiber cement, Everlast composite, Royal & Mastic vinyl, stone veneer; includes trim, soffit, fascia
- Certification: James Hardie Alliance Select
- Price range: $12,000–$25,000 typical; install 4–7 days; 30-year material & labor warranty

## Warranties (The Patriot's Promise)
- Windows: Lifetime material & labor, fully transferable one time then prorated
- Siding: 30-year material & labor
- Entry doors: 15-year material & labor
- Plus: signed timeline agreements, $2M insurance, owner-led accountability

## Financing (GreenSky® program)
- No interest if paid in full within 6 months — no payments required during promo (Plan 2511)
- No interest if paid in full within 15 months — low payments during promo (Plan 4158)
- No interest if paid in full within 18 months — no payments required during promo (Plan 2531)
- Fixed 6.99% APR for 60 months — $19.80/mo per $1,000 financed (Plan 2716)
- Fixed 9.99%–22.99% APR for 120 months (Plan 9991)
- Fixed 9.99% APR for 144 months — $11.94/mo per $1,000 financed (Plan 2749)
- Zero down for most approved homeowners; subject to credit approval; lending through GreenSky program banks
- Apply online: https://projects.greensky.com/merchantloanapplication?apptype=short&merchant=81118420&dealerplan=2716&channel=External-Button-03

## Homeowner Buying Guide

### What window, door & siding projects cost (Tennessee Valley / North Georgia / Northeast Alabama)
- Replacement windows: $700–$2,200 per window installed, depending on size, material and glass package.
- Exterior doors: $3,973–$7,500 installed for entry, patio, French or storm doors.
- Full siding replacement: $12,000–$25,000 typical, including trim, soffit, fascia and house wrap.
- Patriots' Pride gives one honest, itemized number at a free estimate — not a price engineered to drop three times before you sign.

### Windows: vinyl vs. fiberglass
- Vinyl — most cost-effective, virtually maintenance-free, strong modern performance.
- Fiberglass — costs more; stronger, holds paint, expands and contracts less in temperature swings.
- For energy savings in the humid Southeast, choose ENERGY STAR Low-E, argon-filled glass. ENERGY STAR estimates replacing single-pane windows saves about 12% on energy bills on average.
- Styles: double-hung (classic, easy to clean), casement (best seal and airflow), sliders, bay/bow, picture, awning.

### Doors: fiberglass vs. steel
- Steel — most affordable, very secure, but can dent and conducts heat.
- Fiberglass — dent- and rot-resistant, convincing wood-grain looks, better insulation for humidity.
- New entry doors are consistently among the highest-ROI exterior projects in Remodeling magazine's Cost vs. Value report.

### Siding: James Hardie fiber cement vs. vinyl
- Vinyl — budget-friendly, low-maintenance, roughly 20–40 year lifespan.
- James Hardie fiber cement — fire-resistant, won't melt or crack, ColorPlus finish, roughly 30–50 year lifespan; installed to spec by a James Hardie Alliance Select contractor.
- A proper job replaces the full envelope (trim, soffit, fascia, house wrap) and never sides over rot.

### When to replace
- Windows: drafts, fog or condensation between panes, sticking sashes, rising energy bills, rot, outside noise (most last 20–30 years).
- Doors: drafts, hard to open or close, visible damage, security concerns, dated efficiency.
- Siding: warping or cracking, rot or soft spots, fading that won't clean up, moisture or mold, repeated repainting.

### Tax credits & financing
- Federal Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit (25C): currently 30% back on qualifying ENERGY STAR windows (up to $600/year) and exterior doors (up to $500/year) through 2032 — confirm eligibility with a tax advisor.
- Patriots' Pride financing (GreenSky® program): no interest if paid in full within 6, 15 or 18 months; fixed 6.99% APR for 60 months; fixed 9.99% APR for 144 months; 120-month fixed-rate option. Zero down for most approved homeowners. Subject to credit approval.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Windows
**Q: How much does windows replacement cost?**
A: Typical windows projects with Patriots' Pride run $700 – $2,200 / window. Your exact number depends on product line, size and configuration — and you'll get it as one honest, itemized figure at your free estimate, not a price that drops three times before you sign.

**Q: Are replacement windows worth it?**
A: For most homes, yes. New windows are one of the more reliable exterior upgrades for comfort and resale — ENERGY STAR–rated glass cuts the summer heat gain that drives up cooling bills in the Tennessee Valley, drafts and street noise drop noticeably, and windows consistently rank among the better-recouped projects in Remodeling magazine's annual Cost vs. Value report.

**Q: How much can new windows save on energy bills?**
A: It depends on what you're replacing. ENERGY STAR estimates that replacing old single-pane windows with certified windows saves around 12% on energy bills on average; moving from older double-pane to new Low-E, argon-filled glass saves less but still cuts the heat gain that's the number-one comfort complaint in our climate.

**Q: Vinyl or fiberglass windows — which is better?**
A: Both are good; it comes down to budget and priorities. Vinyl is the most cost-effective and virtually maintenance-free, and modern vinyl performs well. Fiberglass costs more but is stronger, holds paint, and expands and contracts less in temperature swings. We're manufacturer-direct on Andersen, MI, Polaris, Vinyl Max, Windsor, Sunrise and HGI, so we match the material to your home and budget instead of pushing one line.

**Q: Double-hung or casement windows — which should I choose?**
A: Double-hung windows (two sashes that slide vertically) are the classic, easy-to-clean choice and suit most homes here. Casement windows crank outward, seal tightly and give the best airflow and view, which makes them great over kitchen sinks or for catching a breeze. Many homes use a mix — we'll walk the options at your free estimate.

**Q: When should I replace my windows? What are the signs?**
A: Common signs: drafts you can feel, fog or condensation between the panes, windows that stick or won't stay open, rising heating and cooling bills, visible rot or water intrusion, and noticeable outside noise. Most windows last 20–30 years; older single-pane and early vinyl units are usually first to go.

**Q: Do new windows add value to my home?**
A: Yes — energy-efficient windows are a common buyer expectation and one of the higher-return exterior improvements in resale studies. Beyond resale, they lower operating costs and improve comfort the whole time you own the home.

**Q: Is there a tax credit for energy-efficient windows?**
A: Currently, the federal Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit (25C) offers 30% back on qualifying ENERGY STAR windows — up to $600 a year — through 2032. We'll point you to the certified product lines that qualify; confirm your eligibility with a tax advisor.

**Q: Will you install windows I bought elsewhere?**
A: Yes. We install owner-supplied windows to manufacturer specification — something most companies refuse to do because they only install what they sell.

**Q: How long does a windows project take?**
A: Most windows installations are completed in 1–2 days typical, depending on the size of the job. We commit to your dates in a signed timeline agreement.

**Q: What warranty comes with windows?**
A: Our windows carry a lifetime (material & labor) warranty, backed in writing and owner-accountable, with $2M insurance on every job.

**Q: Do I need a permit, and do you handle it?**
A: When your project requires a permit, we pull it through your local building department as part of full service — including any added review some historic-district neighborhoods require.

**Q: Do you offer financing?**
A: Yes — through GreenSky®: no interest if paid in full within 6, 15 or 18 months, or fixed rates from 6.99% APR with terms up to 144 months. Most homeowners qualify with zero down.


### Doors
**Q: How much does doors replacement cost?**
A: Typical doors projects with Patriots' Pride run $3,973 – $7,500. Your exact number depends on product line, size and configuration — and you'll get it as one honest, itemized figure at your free estimate, not a price that drops three times before you sign.

**Q: Are new exterior doors worth it?**
A: A new entry door is one of the best-recouped projects in Remodeling magazine's Cost vs. Value report — it upgrades curb appeal, security and the seal against Southeast heat and humidity in a single move, and it's usually a one-day install.

**Q: Fiberglass or steel entry doors — which is better?**
A: Steel is the most affordable and very secure, but it can dent and conducts heat. Fiberglass costs a bit more but resists dents and rot, convincingly mimics wood grain, and insulates better — a strong fit for our humidity. We install ProVia and Andersen in both, so you can compare in person.

**Q: Do new doors improve energy efficiency and security?**
A: Yes. Modern entry doors use insulated fiberglass or steel cores with weatherstripping that actually seals, cutting drafts and air leaks. Upgraded locksets and multi-point hardware also improve security over a worn, poorly fitted old door.

**Q: Do I need a storm door too?**
A: Not always, but a storm door adds an extra weather and energy buffer, lets you open up for ventilation, and protects your main door's finish from sun and rain. We can include one or leave it off — your call.

**Q: What is the Hometown Hero discount?**
A: As a veteran-owned company we take 50% off any exterior door for active military, veterans, police, fire, EMS and dispatchers. Just mention HERO at your free estimate.

**Q: Will you install a door I bought elsewhere?**
A: Yes. We install owner-supplied doors to manufacturer specification — most companies won't touch product they didn't sell, but we will.

**Q: How long does a doors project take?**
A: Most doors installations are completed in 1–2 days, depending on the size of the job. We commit to your dates in a signed timeline agreement.

**Q: What warranty comes with doors?**
A: Our doors carry a lifetime (material & labor) warranty, backed in writing and owner-accountable, with $2M insurance on every job.

**Q: Do I need a permit, and do you handle it?**
A: When your project requires a permit, we pull it through your local building department as part of full service — including any added review some historic-district neighborhoods require.

**Q: Do you offer financing?**
A: Yes — through GreenSky®: no interest if paid in full within 6, 15 or 18 months, or fixed rates from 6.99% APR with terms up to 144 months. Most homeowners qualify with zero down.


### Siding
**Q: How much does siding replacement cost?**
A: Typical siding projects with Patriots' Pride run $12,000 – $25,000 typical. Your exact number depends on product line, size and configuration — and you'll get it as one honest, itemized figure at your free estimate, not a price that drops three times before you sign.

**Q: James Hardie fiber cement or vinyl siding — which is better?**
A: Vinyl is the budget-friendly, low-maintenance option and looks good. James Hardie fiber cement costs more but is far more durable — fire-resistant, won't melt or crack, and its ColorPlus finish holds color against Southeast sun, humidity and storms. As a James Hardie Alliance Select contractor we install it to warranty spec; we also offer Everlast composite and Royal and Mastic vinyl.

**Q: Is fiber cement siding worth the extra cost?**
A: For many homes here, yes. Fiber cement resists heat, moisture, impact and fire, holds its color far longer than vinyl, and typically lasts 30–50 years — and exterior cladding is consistently one of the higher-return projects in resale studies. If budget is tight, quality vinyl is still a solid choice and we'll price both.

**Q: How long does siding last?**
A: Quality vinyl typically lasts 20–40 years and James Hardie fiber cement 30–50 years when installed correctly — which is why we install to manufacturer spec with proper fastening, clearances and a full house-wrap system rather than siding over problems.

**Q: When should I replace my siding? What are the signs?**
A: Warning signs include warping, cracking or buckling panels, rot or soft spots, fading that no longer cleans up, moisture or mold inside, rising energy bills, and needing to repaint every few years. If you're patching the same areas repeatedly, full replacement is usually the better value.

**Q: Does new siding add value to my home?**
A: Yes — re-siding is one of the higher-return exterior projects in resale studies and it dramatically changes curb appeal, while sealing the envelope to improve comfort and lower energy costs.

**Q: Do you repair siding or only replace it?**
A: We do full-replacement siding only. We don't side over rot or patch failing systems — we replace the full envelope including trim, soffit, fascia and house wrap so it's sealed and warrantied as one system.

**Q: How long does a siding project take?**
A: Most siding installations are completed in 4–7 days, depending on the size of the job. We commit to your dates in a signed timeline agreement.

**Q: What warranty comes with siding?**
A: Our siding carry a lifetime (material & labor) warranty, backed in writing and owner-accountable, with $2M insurance on every job.

**Q: Do I need a permit, and do you handle it?**
A: When your project requires a permit, we pull it through your local building department as part of full service — including any added review some historic-district neighborhoods require.

**Q: Do you offer financing?**
A: Yes — through GreenSky®: no interest if paid in full within 6, 15 or 18 months, or fixed rates from 6.99% APR with terms up to 144 months. Most homeowners qualify with zero down.

- Friday–Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM
- Sunday: Closed

## Service Process
1. Free in-home estimate — measure openings, walk options, one honest itemized number
2. Signed timeline agreement; permits pulled as part of full service
3. Expert installation by 4th-generation, manufacturer-direct crew
4. Final walk-through and clean site; warranty in hand

## Customer Reviews (5.0★, 118 Google reviews)
- "We can't say enough about the quality of work and product. From energy efficiency that's already noticeable in our first electric bill, to aesthetic beauty and home safety — we are beyond pleased." — AMANDA TURNER
- "I got estimates from four companies. Patriots' Pride was the least expensive by far. The 7400 series windows were built and installed in less than four weeks. Highly recommend, especially for veterans." — BILL SCOTT
- "Hardie board on our entire house. FANTASTIC job at an unbelievably low price. Ashton was onsite every day. Above all — unquestionable integrity. They earn the name Patriot's PRIDE." — WALT CALDWELL
- "True craftsmanship is a rarity. As an engineer, I notice detail — and there wasn't a thing they could pull over my eyes. So refreshing. We'd recommend Patriots to everyone we know." — BOBBIE BURKE
- "Kenny gave us all our options for windows and doorwalls, introduced the crew on install day, and did a walk-through after. A few small issues were addressed right away. 100% recommend." — HOLLY LEVERTON

## Contact for Estimates
- Free, no-obligation estimates for windows, doors and siding
- Online: https://www.patriotspridewindows.com/get-estimate/
- Phone: (423) 830-7138
